serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of pasta ai funghi e salsiccia is approximately 400 g, comprising boiled pasta, sautéed pork sausage, mushrooms, and cream sauce plated together.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — component estimation from cooked pasta (FDC #168936), Italian pork sausage (FDC #174608), sautéed mushrooms (FDC #169251), and heavy cream (FDC #170859)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for pasta ai funghi e salsiccia exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Values estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FDC SR Legacy entries: cooked pasta (#168936), Italian pork sausage (#174608), sautéed mushrooms (#169251), and heavy cream (#170859). Input per_100g values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are broadly consistent with this component estimate and have been retained with medium confidence. Energy 187.5 kcal, protein 7.5 g, fat 11.25 g, carbohydrate 13.75 g per 100 g.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pasta ai funghi e salsiccia. GI estimated at 45 based on cooked white pasta GI range of 44–50 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Fat and protein from cream and pork sausage further attenuate glycaemic response, supporting a GI at the lower end of the pasta range. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (45 × 12.5 g/100g × 400g/100) / 100 = (45 × 50g) / 100 = 22.5, using net available carbohydrate (13.75 − 1.25 = 12.5 g per 100 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100 g; processed pork sausage contributes variable and potentially high sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 200 g or fewer, request reduced-sodium sausage, and avoid adding extra salt or parmesan cheese, as a full 400 g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium — 52% of the WHO daily limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined white pasta contributes 13.75 g carbohydrate per 100 g with only 1.25 g dietary fibre, producing a moderate glycaemic load of approximately 22.5 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should substitute wholegrain or legume-based pasta to increase fibre and reduce glycaemic impact, limit portion to 200–250 g, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to moderate post-meal blood glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 5.0 g per 100 g from cream and pork sausage; trans fat 0.13 g per 100 g; cholesterol 30 mg per 100 g; sodium 300 mg per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat this dish as an occasional food only: the combination of saturated fat from cream and pork sausage (20 g per 400 g serving), trans fat, and high sodium collectively elevates LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk, and the dish should be replaced with a lean-protein, olive-oil-based pasta preparation for regular consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Processed pork sausage is high in saturated fat and arachidonic acid (omega-6), both of which promote inflammatory pathways relevant to RA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or minimise processed pork sausage in this dish due to its pro-inflammatory saturated fat and arachidonic acid content, and consider substituting with chicken breast or a plant-based protein to reduce inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pasta ai Funghi e Salsiccia is an Italian pasta dish featuring homemade-style pork sausage and sautéed mushrooms in a light cream sauce, commonly served in Italian restaurants in Singapore. The pasta — typically spaghetti, penne, or rigatoni — is boiled al dente and tossed with a sauce made by browning crumbled pork sausage, sautéing sliced mushrooms (often button or cremini) with garlic and onion in olive oil, then finishing with heavy cream and grated parmesan. The result is a rich, savoury, and mildly earthy dish with a creamy coating and umami depth from both the sausage and mushrooms. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 187.5 kcal, 7.5 g protein, 11.25 g fat (5.0 g saturated), and 13.75 g carbohydrate with 1.25 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 300 mg per 100 g, making a full 400 g restaurant serving a moderate-to-high sodium meal (~1200 mg). Clinically, the dish is of moderate concern for type 2 diabetes due to refined pasta carbohydrates and moderate glycaemic load of 22.5 per serving; of moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium from processed sausage and cheese; and of high concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and trans fat from cream and pork sausage. It is not suitable for halal or kosher diets. The dish is popular in Singapore's Italian casual-dining segment and is commonly listed on GrabFood menus under the name AI Funghi E Salsiccia.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for pasta ai funghi e salsiccia exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component analysis of approved USDA FDC SR Legacy sources (cooked pasta #168936, Italian pork sausage #174608, sautéed mushrooms #169251, heavy cream #170859) and are broadly consistent with the input ai_generated values (confidence 0.7). Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation basis and the variable composition of restaurant preparations.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite pasta dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-17. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite dish preparation are not available in approved sources as of 2026-04-17; USDA FDC SR Legacy and Singapore HPB Foodo consulted and confirmed unavailability for this composite preparation. trans_fat_g 0.13 g per 100 g is retained from input; small residual trans fat is plausible from cream and processed pork sausage. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on cooked white pasta GI range (44–50) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283); fat and protein from cream and sausage attenuate glycaemic response. GL per serving = 22.5 using net available carbohydrate (13.75 − 1.25 = 12.5 g/100g × 400g serving). Dish is served in Singapore Italian casual-dining restaurants and listed on GrabFood. Input provenance was ai_generated with confidence 0.7; values cross-checked against USDA component estimates and retained. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule does not apply. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 and FoodEx2 A0CKP used as closest_parent matches with honest gap notes; no codes invented.
